contents.value:
```xml
<person>
  <name>John</name>
  <age>30</age>
</person>
```
**Elements** are the building blocks of XML. They contain text, attributes, and/or child elements. Tags are case-sensitive.

```xml
<item id="123" type="book" available="true">
```
**Attributes** provide metadata. Values must always be quoted. Names must be unique per element.

```xml
<root xmlns:app="http://example.com/app"
      xmlns:db="http://example.com/db">
  <app:config>value</app:config>
  <db:table>users</db:table>
</root>
```
**Namespaces** qualify names with a URI to avoid conflicts between vocabularies.

```xml
<!-- This is a comment -->
<!-- TODO: fix this section -->
```
**Comments** are ignored by parsers. Cannot contain `--` within the body.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
```
**XML declaration** specifies version and encoding. Must be the very first thing in the document.

```xml
<    <
>    >
&   &
"  "
'  '
©  (c)  numeric entity
© (c) hex entity
```
**Entities** represent special characters. Five predefined, plus numeric/hex references.

```xml
<!DOCTYPE note [
  <!ELEMENT note (to,from,body)>
  <!ELEMENT to (#PCDATA)>
  <!ELEMENT from (#PCDATA)>
  <!ELEMENT body (#PCDATA)>
  <!ATTLIST note priority CDATA "normal">
]>
```
**DTD** (Document Type Definition) defines the structure and allowed elements of an XML document.

```xml
<xs:schema xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema">
  <xs:element name="person">
    <xs:complexType>
      <xs:sequence>
        <xs:element name="name" type="xs:string"/>
        <xs:element name="age" type="xs:integer"/>
      </xs:sequence>
    </xs:complexType>
  </xs:element>
</xs:schema>
```
**XSD** (XML Schema Definition) validates XML structure with data types. More powerful than DTD.

```xml

``` **XSLT** transforms XML into other formats (HTML, text, different XML).

```xml
<?xml-stylesheet type="text/xsl" href="style.xsl"?>
<?xml-model href="schema.xsd"?>
```
**Processing instructions** `<?target data?>` provide instructions to the application processing the XML.

```xml
<!-- Well-formed XML rules: -->
<!-- 1. Single root element -->
<!-- 2. Tags properly nested and closed -->
<!-- 3. Attribute values quoted -->
<!-- 4. Case-sensitive tags -->
<!-- 5. Special chars escaped -->
```
**Well-formed** XML follows syntax rules. All XML must be well-formed; valid XML also conforms to a schema.

```xml
``` **Self-closing tags** use `/>` for elements with no content. Equivalent to `

`.

```xml
<html xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">
  <head><title>Page</title></head>
  <body>Content</body>
</html>
```
**Default namespace** `xmlns="..."` applies to the element and all descendants without a prefix.

```xml
xmlns="http://default.namespace"
xmlns:prefix="http://custom.namespace"
```
**xmlns** declares a namespace binding. Default (no prefix) or prefixed (e.g., `xmlns:xsl`).

```xml
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<catalog>
  <book id="1">
    <title>XML Developer Guide</title>
  </book>
</catalog>
```
**Root element** — Every XML document must have exactly one root element that contains all others.

```xml
<paragraph>
  This is <bold>mixed</bold> content with
  <link href="#">inline elements</link>.
</paragraph>
```
**Mixed content** means an element contains both text and child elements. Common in document-oriented XML.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="ISO-8859-1"?>
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-16"?>
```
**Encoding** — UTF-8 is the default and recommended. Specify in the XML declaration if different.
```xml John & Jane d]]> ``` **PCDATA** is parsed text where entities are expanded. CDATA is unparsed literal text.

```xml
<!-- SAX (Simple API for XML) -->
<!-- Event-driven, streaming parser -->
<!-- Low memory: processes one element at a time -->
<!-- Events: startElement, endElement, characters -->
<!-- Best for: large files, one-pass processing -->
```
**SAX** is an event-driven XML parser. Reads sequentially with low memory usage. Ideal for large files.

```xml
<!-- DOM (Document Object Model) -->
<!-- Loads entire document into memory as a tree -->
<!-- Allows random access and modification -->
<!-- Higher memory usage -->
<!-- Best for: small/medium files, complex queries -->
```
**DOM** parser loads the complete XML tree into memory. Supports random access and manipulation.

```xml
<rss version="2.0">
  <channel>
    <title>My Blog</title>
    <link>https://example.com</link>
    <item>
      <title>Post Title</title>
      <description>Summary</description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 01 Jan 2024 00:00:00 GMT</pubDate>
    </item>
  </channel>
</rss>
```
**RSS** (Really Simple Syndication) is an XML format for content feeds.

```xml
<feed xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom">
  <title>My Feed</title>
  <entry>
    <title>Entry Title</title>
    <link href="https://example.com/post"/>
    <updated>2024-01-01T00:00:00Z</updated>
    <content type="html">Content here</content>
  </entry>
</feed>
```
**Atom** is a modern XML feed format. More strictly defined than RSS.

```xml
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="100" height="100">
  <circle cx="50" cy="50" r="40" fill="red"/>
  <text x="50" y="55" text-anchor="middle">Hi</text>
</svg>
```
**SVG** (Scalable Vector Graphics) is an XML-based format for vector images.

```xml
<soap:Envelope xmlns:soap="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/">
  <soap:Header/>
  <soap:Body>
    <GetPrice xmlns="http://example.com">
      <Item>Apple</Item>
    </GetPrice>
  </soap:Body>
</soap:Envelope>
```
**SOAP** is an XML protocol for web services. Uses Envelope, Header, Body structure.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<urlset xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9">
  <url>
    <loc>https://example.com/</loc>
    <lastmod>2024-01-01</lastmod>
    <priority>1.0</priority>
  </url>
</urlset>
```
**Sitemap** XML helps search engines discover and index website pages.

```xml
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0">
  <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>
  <groupId>com.example</groupId>
  <artifactId>my-app</artifactId>
  <version>1.0.0</version>
  <dependencies>
    <dependency>
      <groupId>junit</groupId>
      <artifactId>junit</artifactId>
      <version>4.13</version>
    </dependency>
  </dependencies>
</project>
```
**POM** (pom.xml) is Maven's project configuration. Defines dependencies, plugins, and build settings.

```xml
<web-app xmlns="http://xmlns.jcp.org/xml/ns/javaee" version="4.0">
  <servlet>
    <servlet-name>MyServlet</servlet-name>
    <servlet-class>com.example.MyServlet</servlet-class>
  </servlet>
  <servlet-mapping>
    <servlet-name>MyServlet</servlet-name>
    <url-pattern>/api/*</url-pattern>
  </servlet-mapping>
</web-app>
```
**web.xml** is the Java web application deployment descriptor.

```xml
<manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
    package="com.example.myapp">
  <application android:label="My App">
    <activity android:name=".MainActivity">
      <intent-filter>
        <action android:name="android.intent.action.MAIN"/>
        <category android:name="android.intent.category.LAUNCHER"/>
      </intent-filter>
    </activity>
  </application>
</manifest>
```
**AndroidManifest.xml** declares app components, permissions, and metadata.

```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```
**.csproj** is the MSBuild project file for .NET applications. Defines framework, dependencies, and build settings.

```xml
<!-- XSD data types: -->
<xs:element name="name" type="xs:string"/>
<xs:element name="age" type="xs:integer"/>
<xs:element name="price" type="xs:decimal"/>
<xs:element name="active" type="xs:boolean"/>
<xs:element name="created" type="xs:dateTime"/>
<xs:element name="id" type="xs:ID"/>
```
**XSD types** — `xs:string`, `xs:integer`, `xs:decimal`, `xs:boolean`, `xs:dateTime`, `xs:ID`, and many more.

```xpath
child::book          # direct children
parent::*            # parent element
ancestor::catalog    # all ancestors
descendant::title    # all descendants
following-sibling::* # following siblings  
preceding::*         # all preceding nodes
attribute::id        # attributes (or @id)
self::node()         # current node
```
**XPath axes** specify the direction and scope of node selection in the document tree.

```xpath
count(//book)            # count nodes
sum(//price)             # sum numeric values
string-length(title)     # string length
concat(first, ' ', last) # concatenate
contains(title, 'XML')   # substring check
normalize-space(text())  # trim whitespace
not(@hidden)             # boolean negation
```
**XPath functions** perform calculations and string operations on XML data.

```xml
<xsl:value-of select="title"/>
<xsl:value-of select="@id"/>
<xsl:value-of select="count(item)"/>
```
**xsl:value-of** outputs the text value of an XPath expression in XSLT transformations.

```xml

``` **xsl:for-each** iterates over a node set. Can include `xsl:sort` for ordering.
```xml $ ``` **xsl:if** conditional output. No else — use `xsl:choose` for if-else logic.

```xml
<xsl:choose>
  <xsl:when test="@type='fiction'">Novel</xsl:when>
  <xsl:when test="@type='tech'">Technical</xsl:when>
  <xsl:otherwise>Other</xsl:otherwise>
</xsl:choose>
```
**xsl:choose/when/otherwise** provides if-else-if-else logic in XSLT.

```xml

``` **xsl:template** defines a transformation rule. `match` selects input nodes, content defines output.

```xml
<!-- Schema reference in XML: -->
<root xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
      xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="schema.xsd">

<!-- Or with namespace: -->
<root xmlns="http://example.com"
      xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
      xsi:schemaLocation="http://example.com schema.xsd">
```
**Schema association** — Reference an XSD from within the XML document for validation.
